paper_title: Real‐life evaluation of molecular multiplex IgE test methods in the diagnosis of pollen associated food allergy

methods.description: The process of diagnosing food allergy based on international guidelines, including patient history, skin prick tests (SPTs), and blood sampling.

steps.description: An elaborate patient history was recorded, addressing all aspects of food allergy, including symptoms when ingesting the food suspected to cause an allergic reaction and potential cofactors such as medication, timing, and form of consumption (e.g., raw, cooked, and peeled).
steps.searchable_summary: record detailed patient history for food allergy diagnosis

steps.description: In patients with a clear medical history, SPTs with extracts of suspected causative foods were performed on the patients' forearm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform skin prick tests with food extracts

steps.description: If the patient tested positive in one or more SPTs with food allergens and consented in the participation in the study, a blood sample was taken.
steps.searchable_summary: collect blood sample from patients with positive SPTs

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze allergy data, including sensitivity and specificity calculations, correlation analysis, and data categorization.

steps.description: The data were evaluated using the SPSS Software (Version 27.0, IBM Deutschland GmbH). The data were analyzed in dichotomous (recognition of allergy) and metric (sIgE levels) form.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze allergy data using SPSS software

steps.description: For the calculation of sensitivity and specificity of the ALEX 2 test, the ISAC test was used as reference.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sensitivity and specificity using ISAC as reference

steps.description: For further evaluation, six allergens relevant for PFS and present on both chips were chosen in accordance to their frequency in the study population. For these allergens, the Spearman's correlation coefficient was calculated to compare the measurements of ALEX 2 and ISAC, and data were depicted by scatterplots.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Spearman's correlation for selected allergens

steps.description: The measured sIgE values (ISU-E or kUA/ml, respectively), were divided into the categories negative (<0.3 ISU-E or kUA/ml), low concentration (0.3-0.9 ISU-E or kUA/ml), moderate to high (1-14.9 ISU-E or kUA/ml), and very high concentration (>15 ISU-E or kUA/ml). These ranges are predefined by the manufacturers of the multiplex tests.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize sIgE values into predefined concentration ranges

steps.description: Results of SPTs were put into context with the multiplex test results. For easier interpretation, grouped bar graphs were created.
steps.searchable_summary: create grouped bar graphs for SPT and multiplex test results
